But when Paul and Barnabas learned of this, they tore their clothes and rushed out into the crowd, shouting: вАЬMen, why are you doing this? We too are only men, human like you. We are bringing you good news, telling you to turn from these worthless things to the living God, who made heaven and earth and sea and everything in them.вАЭ

text: вАЬOtsi…Ы n…Ф, …Ы…Ыl…Фk…ФћА w…Фn…Ф …Фkp…Ы ah…Ф…Ы ak…Ф Owusoso Akaa n…ЫћА y…Ыibui…Ы g…Ы al…Фk…Ф ebhui b…Фl…Ф ebu…Ф n…ФћА. K…Ыl…Ы baћАbh…Ф w…Фn…Ф wunu kabha p…Ы k…ЫbaћАk…Ф w…Фn…Ф bag…Ы badal…Ы w…Фn…Ф shu…Ф petee ka≈ЛaћГsi…Ы.
translated: One day, a certain man went to Owusoso Akaa and saw that the path was completely blocked. He looked and saw that a big tree had fallen and covered the whole road.

ref: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
text: Any…Ы teli g…Ыenu k…ЫkpataћА oden…Ф y…Ыm…ФbeћА…Ы, ban…Ф teli al…Фk…Ф y…Ы ededzu akaa buvuћГ…Ф nieduluћГ…Ф g…Ы bekulu as…Ы KeduluћГ GbangbaћГ, …Ыk…Ыl…Ы bedzu y…Ы kibe nyia bep…Ы ebisi bag…Ы bav…Ы Owusoso AkaavuћГ…Ф m…Ы b…Фd…ФћГ.

ref: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
text: G…Ы bediku …Фma m…Ы…Ы, bewu av…Ы kabha buvuћГ…Ф m…Ы g…Ы bazazaћГa. Bag…Ы bazaћГ …Ыk…Ыl…Ы bal…Ы enu Petro, Yohanes, Yakobo n…ЫћА Andrea, Filipo n…ЫћА Toma, Bartolomeo n…ЫћА Mateo, Yakobo, Alfeo y…Ыeyi…Ы n…ЫћА Simon, Ishuah…Ф…Ыm…Ы zazaћГ p…Ыp…Ыelete K…Ыzam…Ыan…Ф, n…ЫћА Yuda Yakobo y…Ыeyi…Ы.
translated: When they arrived there, they saw a crowd around them and the teachers of the law arguing with them. And immediately all the people, when they saw him, were greatly amazed and ran up to him and greeted him. And he asked them, вАЬWhat are you arguing about with them?вАЭ And someone from the crowd answered him, вАЬTeacher, I brought my son to you, for he has a spirit that makes him mute. And whenever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid. So I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they were not able.вАЭ And he answered them, вАЬO faithless generation, how long am I to be with you? How long am I to bear with you? Bring him to me.вАЭ And they brought the boy to him. And when the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth. And Jesus asked his father, вАЬHow long has this been happening to him?вАЭ And he said, вАЬFrom childhood. And it has often cast him into fire and into water, to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.вАЭ Jesus said to him, вАЬвАШIf you canвАЩ! All things are possible for one who believes.вАЭ Immediately the father of the child cried out and said, вАЬI believe; help my unbelief!вАЭ And when Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, вАЬYou mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him and never enter him again.вАЭ And after crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, вАЬHe is dead.вАЭ But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he arose. And when he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, вАЬWhy could we not cast it out?вАЭ And he said to them, вАЬThis kind cannot be driven out by anything but prayer.вАЭ

ref: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
